libraryDescription:
The data.Stack library provides a stack-type data structure.

This module provides the generic struct Stack,which stores stack data,and generic functions such as push and popfor adding and removing data from it.

Note that arrays cannot be handled as elements in data.Stack.If you want to use arrays as elements, usedata.WrappedStack.

struct0.name: Stack<Type>
struct0.description: The stack struct. It stores stack data.

function0.name: clear<Type>
function0.parameters: (Stack<Type> type) stack : The target stack struct.

function1.name: size<Type>
function1.description: Returns the size currently in use.
function1.parameters: (Stack<Type> type) stack : The target stack struct.

function2.name: push<Type>
function2.description: Adds an element to the stack.
function2.parameters:
(Stack<Type> type) stack : The target stack struct. 
(Type type) value : The element to be added.

function3.name: pop<Type>
function3.description: Removes and returns an element from the stack.The extracted element is deleted from the stack.
function3.parameters: (Stack<Type> type) stack : The target stack struct.

function4.name: peek<Type>
function4.description: Returns an element from the stack without removing it.
function4.parameters: (Stack<Type> type) stack : The target stack struct.

function5.name: contains<Type>
function5.description: Determines whether the stack contains the specified element.
function5.parameters:
(Stack<Type> type) stack : The target stack struct. 
(Type type) element : The element whose presence is to be checked.

function6.name: reverse<Type>
function6.description: Reverses the order of all elements held by the stack.
function6.parameters: (Stack<Type> type) stack : The target stack struct.

function7.name: toArray<Type>
function7.description: Converts all elements held by the stack into a one-dimensional array and returns it.The order is such that the element obtained first by pop or peek becomes [0].
function7.parameters: (Stack<Type> type) stack : The target stack struct.
function7.returnValue: (Type[] type) A one-dimensional array containing all elements of the stack.
